title: Mildly Spicy Irish Fish Skillet
description: A comforting and spicy Irish dish featuring fish, soured cream and chive dip, served on a hot stone plate, perfect for a chilly evening. This pescatarian-friendly recipe combines the freshness of fish with the warmth of spices.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the cod fillets with salt and pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the sliced candy peppers and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the diced potato and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the sliced irish sausage and cook until it is browned.
Add the cod fillets to the skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes per side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, mix the soured cream and chopped chives in a bowl.
Remove the skillet from the oven and serve the fish and vegetables on a hot stone plate with the soured cream and chive dip.
